title: Boston police seeking suspect in armed robbery
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek public's help to identify suspect in a Jan. 22 armed robbery in Dorchester.
Suspect allegedly robbed multiple victims at gunpoint near Adams and Whitten Streets around 4:30 p.m.
Anonymous tips can be sent to 1-800-494-TIPS or texted to CRIME (27463).

title: No license yet for World Cup games as Foxborough asks for public safety funding up front
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough officials demand $7.8 million in security funding before issuing World Cup games license at Gillette Stadium.
Host committee proposes payment plan; public safety costs covered within two days post-games, with Krafts backing shortfalls.
Federal security funding delay affects World Cup host cities; Massachusetts to receive over $46 million, pending award.

title: Wu to host first-ever ‘office hours’ for residents to speak with her directly
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu will host an 'office hours' event in Roxbury for Bostonians to share feedback about the city.
The event will feature staff from over a dozen city departments, including schools, police, and housing, to answer questions.
City services such as voter registration and applying for handicap parking will be available during the two-hour event.

title: 7 players Bruins could target at 2026 NHL trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins aim to strengthen their roster ahead of the NHL trade deadline by targeting impact players.
Potential trade targets include Robert Thomas, Conor Garland, Rasmus Ristolainen, and Simon Nemec among others.
Bruins seek long-term roster improvements, focusing on acquiring players with potential rather than short-term rentals.

title: Staff member at special needs residential program in Franklin accused of breaking autistic student’s arm
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Saturs Allison, 27, of Pawtucket, R.I., was indicted for allegedly breaking an autistic student's arm in Franklin, Mass.
The incident occurred in October 2025 at Amego, Inc., a residential program for people with disabilities in Franklin.
Allison pleaded not guilty and is due back in Wrentham District Court for another hearing on April 7.

title: ‘Every run is about defying cancer’: This 18-year-old is running Boston for her childhood friend
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: 18-year-old Amanda Lewis is running the Boston Marathon for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.
Lewis is motivated by the memory of her friend Lily, who died from brain cancer at age 13.
Her goal is to raise money for cancer research and complete the marathon alongside her father.

title: Fall River man sentenced for raping woman when she refused to take naked pictures of young daughter
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Elias Rodrigues Dos Santos Mendes, 55, was sentenced to 8-10 years in prison for rape and child pornography charges.
He raped a woman who refused to take nude photos of her daughter; DNA evidence linked Mendes to the crime.
Mendes, undocumented, faces deportation to Brazil after serving his time, according to the Bristol County DA.

title: What’s next for Patriots’ offense after releasing Stefon Diggs?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots to release Stefon Diggs, freeing up $17 million in cap space for offseason maneuvers.
New England considers WR targets Alec Pierce and A.J. Brown to fill Diggs' departure.
Patriots may also explore drafting or trading for a WR1, with various potential candidates.

title: Each mile is for her miracle: This Granby mom is running the Boston Marathon with her daughter in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brianna Poehler from Granby, Mass. will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Boston Children's Hospital's Miles for Miracles.
Her daughter, a liver transplant survivor, received life-saving treatment at Boston Children's Hospital at 11 months old.
Poehler aims to raise funds for pediatric research and care, honoring her daughter's recovery and medical team's support.

title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: More than 350 readers responded to a Boston.com query on U.S. strikes on Iran; 54% supported the strikes.
Supporters cite Iran's role in regional conflicts and view outside pressure as necessary to weaken its government.
Opponents fear escalation into broader war and highlight lack of congressional approval for the strikes.
